content.problemStatement:
Chef rented a car for a day.

Usually, the cost of the car is Rs 101010 per km. However, since Chef has booked the car for the whole day, he needs to pay for **at least** 300300300 kms even if the car runs less than 300300300 kms.

If the car ran XXX kms, determine the cost Chef needs to pay.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single integer XXX - denoting the number of kms Chef travelled.


Output Format
For each test case, output the cost Chef needs to pay.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer X, the number of kilometres Chef travelled.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the total cost Chef has to pay (in rupees).

sampleTestCases.explanation: 150 is below the minimum, so charge for 300 km → 3000. 300 exactly → 3000. 1000 > 300 → 1000*10 = 10000.
Even though Chef travelled only 1 km, the minimum charge is for 300 km, costing 3000 rupees.

solution.approach: For each test case we need to charge Chef for at least 300 kilometres. The actual kilometres charged is max(X, 300). The rate is Rs 10 per kilometre, so the answer is max(X, 300) * 10. This is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    t = int(data[0])
    out = []
    for i in range(1, t+1):
        x = int(data[i])
        km = x if x > 300 else 300
        out.append(str(km * 10))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
